pain_point_label: Poor Donor Stewardship and Impact Reporting

matches.match_rationale: Donor stewardship is fundamentally a communication problem — the institution fails to produce thank-you letters, impact narratives, and annual reports that make donors feel valued. AI text generation can draft personalized thank-you correspondence, impact stories tied to specific gifts, and templated annual impact reports at scale, eliminating the bottleneck of staff bandwidth. For a multi-site operator with hundreds of donors across campuses, personalizing these communications manually is impractical without AI.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A system that pulls gift data from the CRM and auto-drafts personalized thank-you letters and semi-annual impact updates per donor, requiring only a brief review before sending. Could also generate the narrative sections of an annual impact report from structured program outcome data.

matches.match_rationale: The absence of a stewardship tracking mechanism means tasks like scheduling leadership thank-you calls, sending impact updates at predetermined intervals, and logging stewardship touchpoints simply fall through the cracks. A task automation agent could orchestrate the stewardship workflow end-to-end: trigger drafting at gift receipt, schedule follow-ups, route approval to leadership, and log completion — replacing the missing process entirely. This directly addresses the 'no mechanism to track stewardship tasks' aspect of the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated stewardship pipeline that triggers on gift entry in the donor database: generates a draft communication, routes it to the appropriate campus leader for approval, schedules a 6-month impact check-in, and maintains a stewardship activity log per donor record.

matches.match_rationale: Impact reporting requires distilling program outcomes, student achievements, and financial use-of-funds data into concise donor-facing narratives. Staff at multi-site operators lack time to synthesize reports from multiple program teams, so gifts go unacknowledged. Summarization AI can condense internal program reports, assessment data, and operational updates into brief, donor-readable impact statements, making impact reporting feasible without dedicated development staff.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool that ingests semester program reports and student outcome data from each campus and generates a one-page impact summary per gift category (e.g., scholarship fund, facility improvement), ready for inclusion in donor communications or an annual report.

matches.match_rationale: Donors who feel under-stewarded are at high risk of lapsing; propensity modeling can identify which donors are most likely to give again (or churn) based on their gift history, engagement signals, and stewardship touchpoint data. For a 500+ staff operator managing a donor file of varying sizes, prioritizing stewardship effort toward high-propensity re-givers maximizes fundraising ROI and surfaces at-risk major donor relationships before they go cold.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A donor retention score that flags individuals with high re-gift propensity and low recent stewardship activity, generating a prioritized outreach list for development staff each quarter — ensuring finite stewardship capacity is directed where it matters most.
